name: Telchines
description: The Telchines were mythical sea daemons or sorcerers said to be the first inhabitants of Rhodes. They were skilled metalworkers, particularly in bronze and iron, and were credited with crafting Poseidon's trident and Cronus' sickle. Some accounts depict them as children of Pontus and Gaia, while others claim they were offspring of Thalassa. They were revered for their craftsmanship and knowledge of magic, but also feared for their ability to summon storms, cast evil spells, and shape-shift. Their reputation was mixed, with some associating them with progress and civilization, while others viewed them as malevolent beings who corrupted the land with their sorcery. They were eventually either destroyed by Zeus for flooding the world or driven away by Helios.
subtitle: Craftsmen, sorcerers, and sea daemons
appearance: The Telchines' appearance varied in different accounts. Some described them as having flippers instead of hands, or dog-like heads. Other depictions presented them as skilled artisans, though often with a sinister or unsettling aura. Overall, their physical form was often monstrous or at least distinctly non-human, reflecting their chthonic and elemental nature.
habitat: Primarily associated with the island of Rhodes, but also said to inhabit other islands and coastal regions.
behavior: The Telchines were known for their skill and secrecy. They guarded their knowledge of metalworking and magic jealously. Their behavior ranged from benevolent craftsmanship to malevolent sorcery, depending on the account. They were generally considered to be arrogant and prone to hubris, which ultimately led to their downfall.

keyMyths.title: Crafting Poseidon's Trident
keyMyths.description: The Telchines were renowned for their metalworking skills and are credited with forging Poseidon's powerful trident, a symbol of his dominion over the seas. This showcases their mastery of craft and their connection to the sea gods.

keyMyths.title: Poisoning the Land
keyMyths.description: In some accounts, the Telchines are portrayed as malicious sorcerers who used their knowledge of magic to poison the land and blight the crops, leading to their expulsion or destruction. This highlights the darker aspects of their power and their potential for destruction.

sources.author: Diodorus Siculus
sources.description: A comprehensive historical account that includes descriptions of the Telchines and their association with Rhodes.
sources.text: A comprehensive historical account that includes descriptions of the Telchines and their association with Rhodes.

sources.author: Ovid
sources.description: A collection of mythological tales that portrays the Telchines as malevolent sorcerers.
sources.text: A collection of mythological tales that portrays the Telchines as malevolent sorcerers.

sources.author: Strabo
sources.description: A geographical and historical work that mentions the Telchines' association with metalworking and magic.
